sections.intro:
TheBombermechsare one of theSquadsfromInto The Breachadded in the Advanced Edition. They consist of thePierce Mech,Bombling MechandExchange Mechand are purchasable for four coins.
Despite their explosive nature, the Bombermechs are one of the most tactially-diverse squads due to the Exchange Mech's power and the Pierce Mech's unique damage condition. Every turn will require careful positioning of all three mechs and the Bombling Mech's spawns to maximise your productivity.
sections.Strategies:
- The Walking Bomb is key to everything about the Bombermechs; it can be placed in front of a Pierce Mech to make the latter deal damage, act as swap fodder for the Exchange Mech and then kill weakened enemies. Having more of them also increases your raw damage output, making the Two Bombs upgrade and obtainingSilicaa priority.
  - The Exchange Mech's swap ability can consistently handle multiple threats by swapping them for one another. By swapping an attacking Vek with a well-positioned bombling, it can also reliably make the Vek attack each other. Additionally, if the Exchange Mech is near a ledge with aHornetor is flying usingProspero's ability, they can exchange it with a drownable enemy to instantly kill it. Switching your mech with an enemy leader into environmental damage on theVolcanic Hivecan also trivialise the final mission.Note that Force Swap cannot swap stable enemies or allies, most notablyBurrowers. Use caution when taking on an island with these enemies.
  - The Walking Bombs can reliably block spawns as temporary unit destruction comesafterVek emerging. If the Bombling Mech has two bombs and Silica it can effectively silence the Vek for an entire mission.
  - Look for opportunities to trigger environmental effects such as igniting forests or breaking ice tiles with the bomblings' single point of damage.

full_text:
Bombermechs
“
With a small army of remote explosives and flexible weapons, the tactical options are endless.
„
The
Bombermechs
are one of the
Squads
from
Into The Breach
added in the Advanced Edition. They consist of the
Pierce Mech
,
Bombling Mech
and
Exchange Mech
and are purchasable for four coins.
Despite their explosive nature, the Bombermechs are one of the most tactially-diverse squads due to the Exchange Mech's power and the Pierce Mech's unique damage condition. Every turn will require careful positioning of all three mechs and the Bombling Mech's spawns to maximise your productivity.

Squad Achievements
[
]
Hold the Door
Block 30 Emerging Vek by the end of Island 2.
No Survivors
Have 7 units (any team) die in a single turn.
Powered Blast
Pierce a Walking Bomb with the AP Cannon to kill an Enemy.
Strategies
[
]
The Walking Bomb is key to everything about the Bombermechs; it can be placed in front of a Pierce Mech to make the latter deal damage, act as swap fodder for the Exchange Mech and then kill weakened enemies. Having more of them also increases your raw damage output, making the Two Bombs upgrade and obtaining
Silica
a priority.
The Exchange Mech's swap ability can consistently handle multiple threats by swapping them for one another. By swapping an attacking Vek with a well-positioned bombling, it can also reliably make the Vek attack each other. Additionally, if the Exchange Mech is near a ledge with a
Hornet
or is flying using
Prospero
's ability, they can exchange it with a drownable enemy to instantly kill it. Switching your mech with an enemy leader into environmental damage on the
Volcanic Hive
can also trivialise the final mission.
Note that Force Swap cannot swap stable enemies or allies, most notably
Burrowers
. Use caution when taking on an island with these enemies.
The Walking Bombs can reliably block spawns as temporary unit destruction comes
after
Vek emerging. If the Bombling Mech has two bombs and Silica it can effectively silence the Vek for an entire mission.
Look for opportunities to trigger environmental effects such as igniting forests or breaking ice tiles with the bomblings' single point of damage.
